commands.review_summary:
The PR clamps memory-core's sqlite-vec widen-fallback KNN `k` value to 4096 and adds regression tests for above- and below-ceiling widen queries.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +72. Total +74 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main and v2026.6.10 widen sqlite-vec KNN from `limit * 8` to the full vector table count, while sqlite-vec v0.1.9 rejects k values above 4096. I did not run a live >4096-vector store reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No ClawSweeper repair is queued because the patch has no blocking code finding; the remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real-behavior proof before merge.

target.title: fix(memory-core): clamp widen-fallback kNN k to sqlite-vec 4096 limit
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Once a memory index exceeds **4096 chunks**, `memory_search` fails on any query that triggers the widen fallback in `searchVector`:

```
vector query failed: k value in knn query too large, provided <N> and the limit is 4096
```

To the agent this surfaces as `memory_search timed out after 15s` — the `tools.ts` wrapper trips its timeout after the vector path collapses, so semantic recall silently degrades to keyword/grep fallback with no error shown to the user.

**Root cause.** The widen fallback at `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.ts` re-runs the kNN with `k = vectorCount` — the full vector-table row count — completely unclamped:

```ts
if (vectorCount > candidateLimit) {
  rows = runVectorQuery(vectorCount);   // k can be far above 4096
}
```

sqlite-vec hard-caps `k` at 4096, so for any index larger than that the fallback query throws and the whole vector search path is lost.

(Related: #81172 added event-loop yielding to this same fallback path but did not bound `k`.)

## Fix

Clamp the widen re-query to a new `MAX_VECTOR_KNN_K = 4096` constant:

```ts
rows = runVectorQuery(Math.min(vectorCount, MAX_VECTOR_KNN_K));
```

4096 oversampled candidates is far more than enough to feed top-N hybrid ranking (default `limit * 8` oversample), so there is no meaningful recall loss — the fallback simply stops asking sqlite-vec for more rows than it will ever accept.

## Evidence

- **Reproduction (the failing path):** index >4096 chunks, then run a query whose oversampled first pass (`limit * VECTOR_KNN_OVERSAMPLE_FACTOR`) returns fewer than `limit` rows after source/provenance filtering. That makes `matchingChunkCount > rows.length`, the fallback fires with `k = vectorCount > 4096`, and sqlite-vec rejects it with the error above. With the clamp, `k` is capped at 4096 and the query succeeds.
- **Live observation:** this has been silently degrading semantic recall to grep on a production memory store (index grown past 4096 chunks) since the index crossed that threshold; every `memory_search` there fell into the 15s-timeout path until the identical one-line clamp was applied.
- **CI:** all type/lint/contract/build checks on this PR pass (`check-prod-types`, `check-lint`, `check-test-types`, `build-artifacts`, the full `checks-node-compact-*` matrix, boundary/guard checks). The change is a single bounded `Math.min` on an existing numeric argument — no API or behavior change on indexes ≤4096.

---

_Authored with assistance from Claude Code._
target.author: itsuzef
